Hi!

Thank you for making an admin request on this repo. We strive to make a decision
on these requests within 24 hours.

Please use the text below to add context about this PR, especially if:
- You want to mark packages as broken
- You want to archive a feedstock
- You want to request access to opt-in CI resources

Cheers and thank you for contributing to conda-forge!
-->

## Guidelines for marking packages as broken:

* We prefer to patch the repo data (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/conda-forge-repodata-patches-feedstock))
  instead of marking packages as broken. This alternative workflow makes environments more reproducible.
* Packages with requirements/metadata that are too strict but otherwise work are
  not technically broken and should not be marked as such.
* Packages with missing metadata can be marked as broken on a temporary basis
  but should be patched in the repo data and be marked unbroken later.
* In some cases where the number of users of a package is small or it is used by
  the maintainers only, we can allow packages to be marked broken more liberally.
* We (`conda-forge/core`) try to make a decision on these requests within 24 hours.

What will happen when a package is marked broken?

* Our bots will add the `broken` label to the package. The `main` label will remain on the package and this is normal.
* Our bots will rebuild our repodata patches to remove this package from the repodata.
* In a few hours after the `anaconda.org` CDN picks up the new patches, you will no longer be able to install the package from the `main` channel.


## Checklist:

* [ ] I want to mark a package as broken (or not broken):
  * [ ] Added a description of the problem with the package in the PR description.
  * [ ] Pinged the team for the package for their input.

* [ ] I want to archive a feedstock:
  * [ ] Pinged the team for that feedstock for their input.
  * [ ] Make sure you have opened an issue on the feedstock explaining why it was archived.
  * [ ] Linked that issue in this PR description.
  * [ ] Added links to any other relevant issues/PRs in the PR description.

* [ ] I want to request (or revoke) access to an opt-in CI resource:
  * [ ] Pinged the relevant feedstock team(s)
  * [ ] Added a small description explaining why access is needed

* [ ] I want to copy an artifact following [CFEP-3](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-03.md):
  * [ ] Pinged the relevant feedstock team(s)
  * [ ] Added a reference to the original PR
  * [ ] Posted a link to the conda artifacts
  * [ ] Posted a link to the build logs

* [x] I want to add a package output to a feedstock:
  * [x] Pinged the relevant feedstock team(s)
  * [x] Added a small description of why the output is being added.

<!--
For example if you are trying to mark a `foo` conda package as broken.

  ping @conda-forge/foo

-->

# Description

The [conda-forge tracy](https://github.com/conda-forge/tracy-feedstock/) conflict with `tracy` in bioconda.
The following [PR](https://github.com/conda-forge/tracy-feedstock/pull/2) rename `tracy` into `tracy-profiler`.
We need to add the `tracy-profiler*` output to make it work.

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| Rust            | `@conda-forge/help-rust`      |
| Go              | `@conda-forge/help-go`        |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Zulip chat][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://conda-forge.zulipchat.com

All apologies in advance if your recipe PR does not receive prompt attention.
This is a high volume repository and the reviewers are volunteers. Review times
vary depending on the number of reviewers on a given language team and may be days
or weeks. We are always looking for more staged-recipe reviewers. If you are
interested in volunteering, please contact a member of @conda-forge/core.
We'd love to have the help!
-->

Checklist
- [ ] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [ ] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [ ] Source is from official source.
- [ ] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [ ] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [ ] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [ ] Build number is 0.
- [ ] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).
- [ ] GitHub users listed in the maintainer section have posted a comment confirming they are willing to be listed there.
- [ ] When in trouble, please check our [knowledge base documentation](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html) before pinging a team.

if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

or weeks. We are always looking for more staged-recipe reviewers. If you are
interested in volunteering, please contact a member of @conda-forge/core.
We'd love to have the help!

[Coal](https://github.com/coal-library/coal) is the new name of the hpp-fcl project .
When this package will be released, the hpp-fcl package will be [turned](https://github.com/conda-forge/hpp-fcl-feedstock/pull/123) into a compatibility package.

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/conda-forge/staged-recipes/pulls/comments/1852880191","pull_request_review_id":2452697568,"id":1852880191,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOAn5EZM5ucLU_","diff_hunk":"@@ -0,0 +1,123 @@
+{% set name = "coal" %}
+{% set cppname = "lib" + name %}
+{% set pythonname = name + "-python" %}
+{% set version = "3.0.0" %}
+
+package:
+  name: {{ name|lower }}-split
+  version: {{ version }}
+
+source:
+  url: https://github.com/coal-library/{{ name }}/releases/download/v{{ version }}/{{ name }}-{{ version }}.tar.gz
+  sha256: 6a9cbd4684e907fd16577e5227fbace06ac15ca861c0846dfe5bc81e565fb1e7
+
+build:
+  number: 0
+
+outputs:
+  - name: {{ cppname }}
+    build:
+      run_exports:
+        - {{ pin_subpackage(cppname, max_pin='x.x.x') }}
+    script: build_cppcoal.sh   # [not win]
+    script: build_cppcoal.bat  # [win]
+    requirements:
+      build:
+        - {{ compiler('cxx') }}
+        - {{ stdlib("c") }}
+        - clang  # [win]
+        - cmake
+        - ninja
+        - doxygen
+      host:
+        - eigen
+        - assimp
+        - octomap
+        - libboost-devel
+        - qhull
+        - qhull-static  # [not win]
+      run:
+        - libboost-devel
+        # Since qhullcpp is leaking in header file
+        # consumer will need it to build against coal
+        - qhull-static  # [not win]
+    test:
+      requires:
+        - pkg-config
+      commands:
+        - pkg-config --modversion {{ name }}
+        - test -f $PREFIX/lib/lib{{ name }}${SHLIB_EXT}  # [unix]
+
+  - name: {{ pythonname }}
+    script: build_coal_python.sh   # [not win]
+    script: build_coal_python.bat  # [win]
+    requirements:
+      build:
+        - python                                 # [build_platform != target_platform]
+        - cross-python_{{ target_platform }}     # [build_platform != target_platform]
+        - numpy                                  # [build_platform != target_platform]
+        - {{ compiler('cxx') }}
+        - {{ stdlib("c") }}
+        - clang  # [win]
+        - cmake
+        - ninja
+        - doxygen
+        - pylatexenc  # [not win]
+        - lxml  # [not win]
+      host:
+        - eigen
+        - assimp
+        - octomap
+        - libboost-devel
+        - qhull
+        - qhull-static  # [not win]
+        - python
+        - libboost-python-devel
+        - eigenpy
+        # On Windows, Numpy 2.0 don't allow to export the PY_ARRAY_UNIQUE_SYMBOL.
+        # This has been fixed in Numpy 2.1.
+        - numpy >=2.1      # [win and py>=310]
+        # Unfortunatly, Numpy 2.1 is not distributed with Python 3.9.
+        # We are forced to use Numpy 1 in this case.
+        - numpy >=1.19,<2  # [win and py<310]
+        - numpy            # [not win]
+        - {{ pin_subpackage(cppname, exact=true) }}
+      run:
+        - python
+        - numpy >=2.1                    # [win and py>=310]
+        - {{ pin_compatible('numpy') }}  # [win and py<310]","path":"recipes/coal/meta.yaml","commit_id":"575b5876b229d71c4ac24c76c2510a6f64ae4038","original_commit_id":"575b5876b229d71c4ac24c76c2510a6f64ae4038","user":{"login":"jorisv","id":778707,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjc3ODcwNw==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/778707?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jorisv","html_url":"https://github.com/jorisv","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jorisv/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jorisv/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jorisv/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jorisv/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jorisv/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jorisv/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jorisv/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jorisv/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jorisv/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"Hello,
as explained in the comment, eigenpy can't build with Numpy 2.0 on Windows. We need a new feature added in Numpy 2.1.

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| Rust            | `@conda-forge/help-rust`      |
| Go              | `@conda-forge/help-go`        |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io

All apologies in advance if your recipe PR does not receive prompt attention.
This is a high volume repository and the reviewers are volunteers. Review times
vary depending on the number of reviewers on a given language team and may be days
or weeks. We are always looking for more staged-recipe reviewers. If you are
interested in volunteering, please contact a member of @conda-forge/core.
We'd love to have the help!
-->

[Coal](https://github.com/coal-library/coal) is the new name of the hpp-fcl project .
When this package will be released, the hpp-fcl package will be [turned](https://github.com/conda-forge/hpp-fcl-feedstock/pull/123) into a compatibility package.

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [x] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [x] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).
- [x] GitHub users listed in the maintainer section have posted a comment confirming they are willing to be listed there.
- [x] When in trouble, please check our [knowledge base documentation](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html) before pinging a team.

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/conda-forge/staged-recipes/pulls/comments/1852893601","pull_request_review_id":2452719237,"id":1852893601,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOAn5EZM5ucOmh","diff_hunk":"@@ -0,0 +1,123 @@
+{% set name = "coal" %}
+{% set cppname = "lib" + name %}
+{% set pythonname = name + "-python" %}
+{% set version = "3.0.0" %}
+
+package:
+  name: {{ name|lower }}-split
+  version: {{ version }}
+
+source:
+  url: https://github.com/coal-library/{{ name }}/releases/download/v{{ version }}/{{ name }}-{{ version }}.tar.gz
+  sha256: 6a9cbd4684e907fd16577e5227fbace06ac15ca861c0846dfe5bc81e565fb1e7
+
+build:
+  number: 0
+
+outputs:
+  - name: {{ cppname }}
+    build:
+      run_exports:
+        - {{ pin_subpackage(cppname, max_pin='x.x.x') }}
+    script: build_cppcoal.sh   # [not win]
+    script: build_cppcoal.bat  # [win]
+    requirements:
+      build:
+        - {{ compiler('cxx') }}
+        - {{ stdlib("c") }}
+        - clang  # [win]
+        - cmake
+        - ninja
+        - doxygen
+      host:
+        - eigen
+        - assimp
+        - octomap
+        - libboost-devel
+        - qhull
+        - qhull-static  # [not win]
+      run:
+        - libboost-devel
+        # Since qhullcpp is leaking in header file
+        # consumer will need it to build against coal
+        - qhull-static  # [not win]
+    test:
+      requires:
+        - pkg-config
+      commands:
+        - pkg-config --modversion {{ name }}
+        - test -f $PREFIX/lib/lib{{ name }}${SHLIB_EXT}  # [unix]
+
+  - name: {{ pythonname }}
+    script: build_coal_python.sh   # [not win]
+    script: build_coal_python.bat  # [win]
+    requirements:
+      build:
+        - python                                 # [build_platform != target_platform]
+        - cross-python_{{ target_platform }}     # [build_platform != target_platform]
+        - numpy                                  # [build_platform != target_platform]
+        - {{ compiler('cxx') }}
+        - {{ stdlib("c") }}
+        - clang  # [win]
+        - cmake
+        - ninja
+        - doxygen
+        - pylatexenc  # [not win]
+        - lxml  # [not win]
+      host:
+        - eigen
+        - assimp
+        - octomap
+        - libboost-devel
+        - qhull
+        - qhull-static  # [not win]
+        - python
+        - libboost-python-devel
+        - eigenpy
+        # On Windows, Numpy 2.0 don't allow to export the PY_ARRAY_UNIQUE_SYMBOL.
+        # This has been fixed in Numpy 2.1.
+        - numpy >=2.1      # [win and py>=310]
+        # Unfortunatly, Numpy 2.1 is not distributed with Python 3.9.
+        # We are forced to use Numpy 1 in this case.
+        - numpy >=1.19,<2  # [win and py<310]
+        - numpy            # [not win]
+        - {{ pin_subpackage(cppname, exact=true) }}
+      run:
+        - python

```yaml
  run_exports:
    - numpy >={{ default_abi_level }},<3
```
where `default_abi_level = 1.21`.

It allow then to use numpy 2.0 that is not compatible with eigenpy on Windows.
But since this is also defined in eigenpy, this is maybe not mandatory here Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| Rust            | `@conda-forge/help-rust`      |
| Go              | `@conda-forge/help-go`        |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the

if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io

All apologies in advance if your recipe PR does not receive prompt attention.
This is a high volume repository and the reviewers are volunteers. Review times
vary depending on the number of reviewers on a given language team and may be days
or weeks. We are always looking for more staged-recipe reviewers. If you are
interested in volunteering, please contact a member of @conda-forge/core.
We'd love to have the help!

[Coal](https://github.com/coal-library/coal) is the new name of the hpp-fcl project .
When this package will be released, the hpp-fcl package will be [turned](https://github.com/conda-forge/hpp-fcl-feedstock/pull/123) into a compatibility package.

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [x] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [x] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).
- [x] GitHub users listed in the maintainer section have posted a comment confirming they are willing to be listed there.
- [x] When in trouble, please check our [knowledge base documentation](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html) before pinging a team.

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| Rust            | `@conda-forge/help-rust`      |
| Go              | `@conda-forge/help-go`        |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io

All apologies in advance if your recipe PR does not receive prompt attention.
This is a high volume repository and the reviewers are volunteers. Review times
vary depending on the number of reviewers on a given language team and may be days
or weeks. We are always looking for more staged-recipe reviewers. If you are
interested in volunteering, please contact a member of @conda-forge/core.
We'd love to have the help!
-->

[Coal](https://github.com/coal-library/coal) is the new name of the hpp-fcl project .
When this package will be released, the hpp-fcl package will be [turned](https://github.com/conda-forge/hpp-fcl-feedstock/pull/123) into a compatibility package.

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [x] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [x] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).
- [x] GitHub users listed in the maintainer section have posted a comment confirming they are willing to be listed there.
- [x] When in trouble, please check our [knowledge base documentation](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html) before pinging a team.

+{% set name = "coal" %}
+{% set cppname = "lib" + name %}
+{% set pythonname = name + "-python" %}
+{% set version = "3.0.0" %}
+
+package:
+  name: {{ name|lower }}-split
+  version: {{ version }}
+
+source:
+  url: https://github.com/coal-library/{{ name }}/releases/download/v{{ version }}/{{ name }}-{{ version }}.tar.gz
+  sha256: 6a9cbd4684e907fd16577e5227fbace06ac15ca861c0846dfe5bc81e565fb1e7
+
+build:
+  number: 0
+
+outputs:
+  - name: {{ cppname }}
+    build:
+      run_exports:
+        - {{ pin_subpackage(cppname, max_pin='x.x.x') }}
+    script: build_cppcoal.sh   # [not win]
+    script: build_cppcoal.bat  # [win]
+    requirements:
+      build:
+        - {{ compiler('cxx') }}
+        - {{ stdlib("c") }}
+        - clang  # [win]
+        - cmake
+        - ninja
+        - doxygen
+      host:
+        - eigen
+        - assimp
+        - octomap
+        - libboost-devel
+        - qhull
+        - qhull-static  # [not win]
+      run:
+        - libboost-devel
+        # Since qhullcpp is leaking in header file
+        # consumer will need it to build against coal
+        - qhull-static  # [not win]
+    test:
+      requires:
+        - pkg-config
+      commands:
+        - pkg-config --modversion {{ name }}
+        - test -f $PREFIX/lib/lib{{ name }}${SHLIB_EXT}  # [unix]
+
+  - name: {{ pythonname }}
+    script: build_coal_python.sh   # [not win]
+    script: build_coal_python.bat  # [win]
+    requirements:
+      build:
+        - python                                 # [build_platform != target_platform]
+        - cross-python_{{ target_platform }}     # [build_platform != target_platform]
+        - numpy                                  # [build_platform != target_platform]
+        - {{ compiler('cxx') }}
+        - {{ stdlib("c") }}
+        - clang  # [win]
+        - cmake
+        - ninja
+        - doxygen
+        - pylatexenc  # [not win]
+        - lxml  # [not win]
+      host:
+        - eigen
+        - assimp
+        - octomap
+        - libboost-devel
+        - qhull
+        - qhull-static  # [not win]
+        - python
+        - libboost-python-devel
+        - eigenpy
+        # On Windows, Numpy 2.0 don't allow to export the PY_ARRAY_UNIQUE_SYMBOL.
+        # This has been fixed in Numpy 2.1.
+        - numpy >=2.1      # [win and py>=310]
+        # Unfortunatly, Numpy 2.1 is not distributed with Python 3.9.
+        # We are forced to use Numpy 1 in this case.
+        - numpy >=1.19,<2  # [win and py<310]
+        - numpy            # [not win]","path":"recipes/coal/meta.yaml","commit_id":"575b5876b229d71c4ac24c76c2510a6f64ae4038","original_commit_id":"575b5876b229d71c4ac24c76c2510a6f64ae4038","user":{"login":"jorisv","id":778707,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjc3ODcwNw==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/778707?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jorisv","html_url":"https://github.com/jorisv","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jorisv/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jorisv/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jorisv/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jorisv/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jorisv/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jorisv/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jorisv/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jorisv/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jorisv/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"Anyway, Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| Rust            | `@conda-forge/help-rust`      |
| Go              | `@conda-forge/help-go`        |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io

All apologies in advance if your recipe PR does not receive prompt attention.
This is a high volume repository and the reviewers are volunteers. Review times
vary depending on the number of reviewers on a given language team and may be days
or weeks. We are always looking for more staged-recipe reviewers. If you are
interested in volunteering, please contact a member of @conda-forge/core.
We'd love to have the help!
-->

[Coal](https://github.com/coal-library/coal) is the new name of the hpp-fcl project .
When this package will be released, the hpp-fcl package will be [turned](https://github.com/conda-forge/hpp-fcl-feedstock/pull/123) into a compatibility package.

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [x] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [x] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).
- [x] GitHub users listed in the maintainer section have posted a comment confirming they are willing to be listed there.
- [x] When in trouble, please check our [knowledge base documentation](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html) before pinging a team.

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| Rust            | `@conda-forge/help-rust`      |
| Go              | `@conda-forge/help-go`        |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io

All apologies in advance if your recipe PR does not receive prompt attention.
This is a high volume repository and the reviewers are volunteers. Review times
vary depending on the number of reviewers on a given language team and may be days
or weeks. We are always looking for more staged-recipe reviewers. If you are
interested in volunteering, please contact a member of @conda-forge/core.
We'd love to have the help!
-->

[Coal](https://github.com/coal-library/coal) is the new name of the hpp-fcl project .
When this package will be released, the hpp-fcl package will be [turned](https://github.com/conda-forge/hpp-fcl-feedstock/pull/123) into a compatibility package.

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [x] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [x] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).
- [x] GitHub users listed in the maintainer section have posted a comment confirming they are willing to be listed there.
- [x] When in trouble, please check our [knowledge base documentation](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html) before pinging a team.

PR has been triggered in an effort to update [**numpy2**](https://conda-forge.org/status/migration/?name=numpy2). Notes and instructions for merging this PR: 1. Please merge the PR only after the tests have passed. 2. Feel free to push to the bot's branch to update this PR if needed. **Please note that if you close this PR we presume that the feedstock has been rebuilt, so if you are going to perform the rebuild yourself don't close this PR until the your rebuild has been merged.**
Here are some more details about this specific migrator: > > TL;DR: The way we build against numpy has changed as of numpy 2.0. This bot > PR has updated the recipe to account for the changes (see below for details). > > The biggest change is that we no longer need to use the oldest available numpy > version at build time in order to support old numpy version at runtime - numpy > will by default use a compatible ABI for the oldest still-supported numpy versions. > > Additionally, we no longer need to use `{{ pin_compatible("numpy") }}` as a > run requirement - this has been handled for more than two years now by a > run-export on the numpy package itself. The migrator will therefore remove > any occurrences of this. > > However, you will still need to add the lower bound for the numpy version, > in line with what the upstream package requires. The default lower bound from > the run-export is `>=1.19`; if your package needs a newer version than that, > please add `numpy >=x.y` under `run:`. > > Finally, by default, building against numpy 2.0 will assume that the package > is compatible with numpy 2.0, which is not necessarily the case. You should > check that the upstream package explicitly supports numpy 2.0, otherwise you > need to add a `- numpy <2.0dev0` run requirement until that happens (check numpy > issue 26191 for an overview of the most important packages). > > ### To-Dos: > * [ ] Match run-requirements for numpy (i.e. check upstream `pyproject.toml` or however the project specifies numpy compatibility) > * If upstream is not yet compatible with numpy 2.0, add `numpy <2.0dev0` upper bound under `run:`. > * If upstream is already compatible with numpy 2.0, double-check their supported numpy versions. > * If upstream requires a minimum numpy version newer than 1.19, you need to add `numpy >=x.y` under `run:`. > * [ ] Remove any remaining occurrences of `{{ pin_compatible("numpy") }}` that the bot may have missed. > > PS. If the build does not compile anymore, this is almost certainly a sign that > the upstream project is not yet ready for numpy 2.0; do not close this PR until > a version compatible with numpy 2.0 has been released upstream and on this > feedstock (in the meantime, you can keep the bot from reopening this PR in > case of git conflicts by marking it as a draft).

PR has been triggered in an effort to update [**numpy2**](https://conda-forge.org/status/migration/?name=numpy2). Notes and instructions for merging this PR: 1. Please merge the PR only after the tests have passed. 2. Feel free to push to the bot's branch to update this PR if needed. **Please note that if you close this PR we presume that the feedstock has been rebuilt, so if you are going to perform the rebuild yourself don't close this PR until the your rebuild has been merged.**
Here are some more details about this specific migrator: > > TL;DR: The way we build against numpy has changed as of numpy 2.0. This bot > PR has updated the recipe to account for the changes (see below for details). > > The biggest change is that we no longer need to use the oldest available numpy > version at build time in order to support old numpy version at runtime - numpy > will by default use a compatible ABI for the oldest still-supported numpy versions. > > Additionally, we no longer need to use `{{ pin_compatible("numpy") }}` as a > run requirement - this has been handled for more than two years now by a > run-export on the numpy package itself. The migrator will therefore remove > any occurrences of this. > > However, you will still need to add the lower bound for the numpy version, > in line with what the upstream package requires. The default lower bound from > the run-export is `>=1.19`; if your package needs a newer version than that, > please add `numpy >=x.y` under `run:`. > > Finally, by default, building against numpy 2.0 will assume that the package > is compatible with numpy 2.0, which is not necessarily the case. You should > check that the upstream package explicitly supports numpy 2.0, otherwise you > need to add a `- numpy <2.0dev0` run requirement until that happens (check numpy > issue 26191 for an overview of the most important packages). > > ### To-Dos: > * [ ] Match run-requirements for numpy (i.e. check upstream `pyproject.toml` or however the project specifies numpy compatibility) > * If upstream is not yet compatible with numpy 2.0, add `numpy <2.0dev0` upper bound under `run:`. > * If upstream is already compatible with numpy 2.0, double-check their supported numpy versions. > * If upstream requires a minimum numpy version newer than 1.19, you need to add `numpy >=x.y` under `run:`. > * [ ] Remove any remaining occurrences of `{{ pin_compatible("numpy") }}` that the bot may have missed. > > PS. If the build does not compile anymore, this is almost certainly a sign that > the upstream project is not yet ready for numpy 2.0; do not close this PR until > a version compatible with numpy 2.0 has been released upstream and on this > feedstock (in the meantime, you can keep the bot from reopening this PR in > case of git conflicts by marking it as a draft).

{"repository_id":197821489,"push_id":20812937915,"size":12,"distinct_size":12,"ref":"refs/heads/main","head":"8c8af7089eeeee395abd27760cf052163801bc9f","before":"2f5eb1e6e605b3c97f5abe9913f549bed5019790","commits":[{"sha":"d0ab7cc390194eff6ab7772030f8dd3a788edbf8","author":{"email":"75d0c4ad517d85c3745aa112e45f2e479cb18cc2@users.noreply.github.com","name":"regro-cf-autotick-bot"},"message":"Rebuild for numpy 2.0

TL;DR: The way we build against numpy has changed as of numpy 2.0. This bot
PR has updated the recipe to account for the changes (see below for details).

The biggest change is that we no longer need to use the oldest available numpy
version at build time in order to support old numpy version at runtime - numpy
will by default use a compatible ABI for the oldest still-supported numpy versions.

Additionally, we no longer need to use `{{ pin_compatible("numpy") }}` as a
run requirement - this has been handled for more than two years now by a
run-export on the numpy package itself. The migrator will therefore remove
any occurrences of this.

However, you will still need to add the lower bound for the numpy version,
in line with what the upstream package requires. The default lower bound from
the run-export is `>=1.19`; if your package needs a newer version than that,
please add `numpy >=x.y` under `run:`.

Finally, by default, building against numpy 2.0 will assume that the package
is compatible with numpy 2.0, which is not necessarily the case. You should
check that the upstream package explicitly supports numpy 2.0, otherwise you
need to add a `- numpy <2.0dev0` run requirement until that happens (check numpy
issue 26191 for an overview of the most important packages).

### To-Dos:
  * [ ] Match run-requirements for numpy (i.e. check upstream `pyproject.toml` or however the project specifies numpy compatibility)
    * If upstream is not yet compatible with numpy 2.0, add `numpy <2.0dev0` upper bound under `run:`.
    * If upstream is already compatible with numpy 2.0, double-check their supported numpy versions.
    * If upstream requires a minimum numpy version newer than 1.19, you need to add `numpy >=x.y` under `run:`.
  * [ ] Remove any remaining occurrences of `{{ pin_compatible("numpy") }}` that the bot may have missed.

PS. If the build does not compile anymore, this is almost certainly a sign that
the upstream project is not yet ready for numpy 2.0; do not close this PR until
a version compatible with numpy 2.0 has been released upstream and on this
feedstock (in the meantime, you can keep the bot from reopening this PR in
case of git conflicts by marking it as a {"repository_id":398722885,"push_id":20832469117,"size":6,"distinct_size":6,"ref":"refs/heads/main","head":"83759017b3d16cae57230fe8842cd4e179043a0c","before":"85cd8b0dd55c3598af19c8173b2f53eeb25e13e3","commits":[{"sha":"2575f264e4ffb11f089bb5040dacf1cce8b85c76","author":{"email":"75d0c4ad517d85c3745aa112e45f2e479cb18cc2@users.noreply.github.com","name":"regro-cf-autotick-bot"},"message":"Rebuild for numpy 2.0

TL;DR: The way we build against numpy has changed as of numpy 2.0. This bot
PR has updated the recipe to account for the changes (see below for details).

The biggest change is that we no longer need to use the oldest available numpy
version at build time in order to support old numpy version at runtime - numpy
will by default use a compatible ABI for the oldest still-supported numpy versions.

Additionally, we no longer need to use `{{ pin_compatible("numpy") }}` as a
run requirement - this has been handled for more than two years now by a
run-export on the numpy package itself. The migrator will therefore remove
any occurrences of this.

However, you will still need to add the lower bound for the numpy version,
in line with what the upstream package requires. The default lower bound from
the run-export is `>=1.19`; if your package needs a newer version than that,
please add `numpy >=x.y` under `run:`.

Finally, by default, building against numpy 2.0 will assume that the package
is compatible with numpy 2.0, which is not necessarily the case. You should
check that the upstream package explicitly supports numpy 2.0, otherwise you
need to add a `- numpy <2.0dev0` run requirement until that happens (check numpy
issue 26191 for an overview of the most important packages).

### To-Dos:
  * [ ] Match run-requirements for numpy (i.e. check upstream `pyproject.toml` or however the project specifies numpy compatibility)
    * If upstream is not yet compatible with numpy 2.0, add `numpy <2.0dev0` upper bound under `run:`.
    * If upstream is already compatible with numpy 2.0, double-check their supported numpy versions.
    * If upstream requires a minimum numpy version newer than 1.19, you need to add `numpy >=x.y` under `run:`.
  * [ ] Remove any remaining occurrences of `{{ pin_compatible("numpy") }}` that the bot may have missed.

PS. If the build does not compile anymore, this is almost certainly a sign that
the upstream project is not yet ready for numpy 2.0; do not close this PR until
a version compatible with numpy 2.0 has been released upstream and on this
feedstock (in the meantime, you can keep the bot from reopening this PR in
case of git conflicts by marking it as a draft).","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/conda-forge/pycppad-feedstock/commits/2575f264e4ffb11f089bb5040dacf1cce8b85c76"},{"sha":"7e8e8d3d7b23bca2e41a7c2c3cf684af87973400","author":{"email":"75d0c4ad517d85c3745aa112e45f2e479cb18cc2@users.noreply.github.com","name":"regro-cf-autotick-bot"},"message":"MNT: Re-rendered with conda-build 24.9.0, conda-smithy 3.43.0, and conda-forge-pinning 2024.10.21.11.59.29","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/conda-forge/pycppad-feedstock/commits/7e8e8d3d7b23bca2e41a7c2c3cf684af87973400"},{"sha":"654f3106d890c4adc0a652c788f476d788089d26","author":{"email":"d40d6df22368c8380fb0038562cab74a4e7e3bc8@inria.fr","name":"Joris Vaillant"},"message":"Update Numpy path","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/conda-forge/pycppad-feedstock/commits/654f3106d890c4adc0a652c788f476d788089d26"},{"sha":"78b8bffbe70902fcc592b092df23461f5118d23e","author":{"email":"d40d6df22368c8380fb0038562cab74a4e7e3bc8@inria.fr","name":"Joris Vaillant"},"message":"Add numpy version constraints on Windows","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/conda-forge/pycppad-feedstock/commits/78b8bffbe70902fcc592b092df23461f5118d23e"},{"sha":"1891af46880b78169555d3b655e0c431e0659141","author":{"email":"d40d6df22368c8380fb0038562cab74a4e7e3bc8@inria.fr","name":"Joris Vaillant"},"message":"MNT: Re-rendered with conda-build 24.7.1, conda-smithy 3.43.0, and conda-forge-pinning 2024.10.22.08.35.23","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/conda-forge/pycppad-feedstock/commits/1891af46880b78169555d3b655e0c431e0659141"},{"sha":"83759017b3d16cae57230fe8842cd4e179043a0c","author":{"email":"d40d6df22368c8380fb0038562cab74a4e7e3bc8@inria.fr","name":"Joris Vaillant"},"message":"Merge pull request #33 from regro-cf-autotick-bot/rebuild-numpy2-0-1_h967389

{"repository_id":201127259,"push_id":20832863760,"size":6,"distinct_size":6,"ref":"refs/heads/main","head":"d181a48906d18e57a0d60b2120e9706f82ddfad9","before":"6fa776419b5ae2f458219cc7f6a5f29e17619b71","commits":[{"sha":"54ee365bd2eb08bc3015dbc5d99d0c7d0e52f19d","author":{"email":"75d0c4ad517d85c3745aa112e45f2e479cb18cc2@users.noreply.github.com","name":"regro-cf-autotick-bot"},"message":"Rebuild for numpy 2.0

TL;DR: The way we build against numpy has changed as of numpy 2.0. This bot
PR has updated the recipe to account for the changes (see below for details).

The biggest change is that we no longer need to use the oldest available numpy
version at build time in order to support old numpy version at runtime - numpy
will by default use a compatible ABI for the oldest still-supported numpy versions.

Additionally, we no longer need to use `{{ pin_compatible("numpy") }}` as a
run requirement - this has been handled for more than two years now by a
run-export on the numpy package itself. The migrator will therefore remove
any occurrences of this.

However, you will still need to add the lower bound for the numpy version,
in line with what the upstream package requires. The default lower bound from
the run-export is `>=1.19`; if your package needs a newer version than that,
please add `numpy >=x.y` under `run:`.

Finally, by default, building against numpy 2.0 will assume that the package
is compatible with numpy 2.0, which is not necessarily the case. You should
check that the upstream package explicitly supports numpy 2.0, otherwise you
need to add a `- numpy <2.0dev0` run requirement until that happens (check numpy
issue 26191 for an overview of the most important packages).

### To-Dos:
  * [ ] Match run-requirements for numpy (i.e. check upstream `pyproject.toml` or however the project specifies numpy compatibility)
    * If upstream is not yet compatible with numpy 2.0, add `numpy <2.0dev0` upper bound under `run:`.
    * If upstream is already compatible with numpy 2.0, double-check their supported numpy versions.
    * If upstream requires a minimum numpy version newer than 1.19, you need to add `numpy >=x.y` under `run:`.
  * [ ] Remove any remaining occurrences of `{{ pin_compatible("numpy") }}` that the bot may have missed.

PS. If the build does not compile anymore, this is almost certainly a sign that
the upstream project is not yet ready for numpy 2.0; do not close this PR until
a version compatible with numpy 2.0 has been released upstream and on this
feedstock (in the meantime, you can keep the bot from reopening this PR in

Notes and instructions for merging this PR: 1. Please merge the PR only after the tests have passed. 2. Feel free to push to the bot's branch to update this PR if needed. **Please note that if you close this PR we presume that the feedstock has been rebuilt, so if you are going to perform the rebuild yourself don't close this PR until the your rebuild has been merged.**
Here are some more details about this specific migrator: > > TL;DR: The way we build against numpy has changed as of numpy 2.0. This bot > PR has updated the recipe to account for the changes (see below for details). > > The biggest change is that we no longer need to use the oldest available numpy > version at build time in order to support old numpy version at runtime - numpy > will by default use a compatible ABI for the oldest still-supported numpy versions. > > Additionally, we no longer need to use `{{ pin_compatible("numpy") }}` as a > run requirement - this has been handled for more than two years now by a > run-export on the numpy package itself. The migrator will therefore remove > any occurrences of this. > > However, you will still need to add the lower bound for the numpy version, > in line with what the upstream package requires. The default lower bound from > the run-export is `>=1.19`; if your package needs a newer version than that, > please add `numpy >=x.y` under `run:`. > > Finally, by default, building against numpy 2.0 will assume that the package > is compatible with numpy 2.0, which is not necessarily the case. You should > check that the upstream package explicitly supports numpy 2.0, otherwise you > need to add a `- numpy <2.0dev0` run requirement until that happens (check numpy > issue 26191 for an overview of the most important packages). > > ### To-Dos: > * [ ] Match run-requirements for numpy (i.e. check upstream `pyproject.toml` or however the project specifies numpy compatibility) > * If upstream is not yet compatible with numpy 2.0, add `numpy <2.0dev0` upper bound under `run:`. > * If upstream is already compatible with numpy 2.0, double-check their supported numpy versions. > * If upstream requires a minimum numpy version newer than 1.19, you need to add `numpy >=x.y` under `run:`. > * [ ] Remove any remaining occurrences of `{{ pin_compatible("numpy") }}` that the bot may have missed. > > PS. If the build does not compile anymore, this is almost certainly a sign that > the upstream project is not yet ready for numpy 2.0; do not close this PR until > a version compatible with numpy 2.0 has been released upstream and on this > feedstock (in the meantime, you can keep the bot from reopening this PR in > case of git conflicts by marking it as a draft).

{"repository_id":750505322,"push_id":20891461059,"size":6,"distinct_size":6,"ref":"refs/heads/main","head":"dd31a7d900ca48408270911f8b06069c167c1197","before":"b4320b84f219e85dbf501b40f624f104c4864edb","commits":[{"sha":"cbfae42bf9ac39f0ce6c754b102ba650be6b6533","author":{"email":"75d0c4ad517d85c3745aa112e45f2e479cb18cc2@users.noreply.github.com","name":"regro-cf-autotick-bot"},"message":"Rebuild for numpy 2.0

TL;DR: The way we build against numpy has changed as of numpy 2.0. This bot
PR has updated the recipe to account for the changes (see below for details).

The biggest change is that we no longer need to use the oldest available numpy
version at build time in order to support old numpy version at runtime - numpy
will by default use a compatible ABI for the oldest still-supported numpy versions.

Additionally, we no longer need to use `{{ pin_compatible("numpy") }}` as a
run requirement - this has been handled for more than two years now by a
run-export on the numpy package itself. The migrator will therefore remove
any occurrences of this.

However, you will still need to add the lower bound for the numpy version,
in line with what the upstream package requires. The default lower bound from
the run-export is `>=1.19`; if your package needs a newer version than that,
please add `numpy >=x.y` under `run:`.

Finally, by default, building against numpy 2.0 will assume that the package
is compatible with numpy 2.0, which is not necessarily the case. You should
check that the upstream package explicitly supports numpy 2.0, otherwise you
need to add a `- numpy <2.0dev0` run requirement until that happens (check numpy
issue 26191 for an overview of the most important packages).

### To-Dos:
  * [ ] Match run-requirements for numpy (i.e. check upstream `pyproject.toml` or however the project specifies numpy compatibility)
    * If upstream is not yet compatible with numpy 2.0, add `numpy <2.0dev0` upper bound under `run:`.
    * If upstream is already compatible with numpy 2.0, double-check their supported numpy versions.
    * If upstream requires a minimum numpy version newer than 1.19, you need to add `numpy >=x.y` under `run:`.
  * [ ] Remove any remaining occurrences of `{{ pin_compatible("numpy") }}` that the bot may have missed.

PS. If the build does not compile anymore, this is almost certainly a sign that
the upstream project is not yet ready for numpy 2.0; do not close this PR until
a version compatible with numpy 2.0 has been released upstream and on this
feedstock (in the meantime, you can keep the bot from reopening this PR in

Here are some more details about this specific migrator: > > TL;DR: The way we build against numpy has changed as of numpy 2.0. This bot > PR has updated the recipe to account for the changes (see below for details). > > The biggest change is that we no longer need to use the oldest available numpy > version at build time in order to support old numpy version at runtime - numpy > will by default use a compatible ABI for the oldest still-supported numpy versions. > > Additionally, we no longer need to use `{{ pin_compatible("numpy") }}` as a > run requirement - this has been handled for more than two years now by a > run-export on the numpy package itself. The migrator will therefore remove > any occurrences of this. > > However, you will still need to add the lower bound for the numpy version, > in line with what the upstream package requires. The default lower bound from > the run-export is `>=1.19`; if your package needs a newer version than that, > please add `numpy >=x.y` under `run:`. > > Finally, by default, building against numpy 2.0 will assume that the package > is compatible with numpy 2.0, which is not necessarily the case. You should > check that the upstream package explicitly supports numpy 2.0, otherwise you > need to add a `- numpy <2.0dev0` run requirement until that happens (check numpy > issue 26191 for an overview of the most important packages). > > ### To-Dos: > * [ ] Match run-requirements for numpy (i.e. check upstream `pyproject.toml` or however the project specifies numpy compatibility) > * If upstream is not yet compatible with numpy 2.0, add `numpy <2.0dev0` upper bound under `run:`. > * If upstream is already compatible with numpy 2.0, double-check their supported numpy versions. > * If upstream requires a minimum numpy version newer than 1.19, you need to add `numpy >=x.y` under `run:`. > * [ ] Remove any remaining occurrences of `{{ pin_compatible("numpy") }}` that the bot may have missed. > > PS. If the build does not compile anymore, this is almost certainly a sign that > the upstream project is not yet ready for numpy 2.0; do not close this PR until > a version compatible with numpy 2.0 has been released upstream and on this > feedstock (in the meantime, you can keep the bot from reopening this PR in > case of git conflicts by marking it as a draft).

Notes and instructions for merging this PR: 1. Please merge the PR only after the tests have passed. 2. Feel free to push to the bot's branch to update this PR if needed. **Please note that if you close this PR we presume that the feedstock has been rebuilt, so if you are going to perform the rebuild yourself don't close this PR until the your rebuild has been merged.**
Here are some more details about this specific migrator: > > TL;DR: The way we build against numpy has changed as of numpy 2.0. This bot > PR has updated the recipe to account for the changes (see below for details). > > The biggest change is that we no longer need to use the oldest available numpy > version at build time in order to support old numpy version at runtime - numpy > will by default use a compatible ABI for the oldest still-supported numpy versions. > > Additionally, we no longer need to use `{{ pin_compatible("numpy") }}` as a > run requirement - this has been handled for more than two years now by a > run-export on the numpy package itself. The migrator will therefore remove > any occurrences of this. > > However, you will still need to add the lower bound for the numpy version, > in line with what the upstream package requires. The default lower bound from > the run-export is `>=1.19`; if your package needs a newer version than that, > please add `numpy >=x.y` under `run:`. > > Finally, by default, building against numpy 2.0 will assume that the package > is compatible with numpy 2.0, which is not necessarily the case. You should > check that the upstream package explicitly supports numpy 2.0, otherwise you > need to add a `- numpy <2.0dev0` run requirement until that happens (check numpy > issue 26191 for an overview of the most important packages). > > ### To-Dos: > * [ ] Match run-requirements for numpy (i.e. check upstream `pyproject.toml` or however the project specifies numpy compatibility) > * If upstream is not yet compatible with numpy 2.0, add `numpy <2.0dev0` upper bound under `run:`. > * If upstream is already compatible with numpy 2.0, double-check their supported numpy versions. > * If upstream requires a minimum numpy version newer than 1.19, you need to add `numpy >=x.y` under `run:`. > * [ ] Remove any remaining occurrences of `{{ pin_compatible("numpy") }}` that the bot may have missed. > > PS. If the build does not compile anymore, this is almost certainly a sign that > the upstream project is not yet ready for numpy 2.0; do not close this PR until > a version compatible with numpy 2.0 has been released upstream and on this > feedstock (in the meantime, you can keep the bot from reopening this PR in > case of git conflicts by marking it as a draft).

{"repository_id":750505268,"push_id":20927375857,"size":5,"distinct_size":5,"ref":"refs/heads/main","head":"f7ec66aff329f8b2a43db476dbcf6028000a78a9","before":"1fedd1071c02204f6d001be94dd213034209941c","commits":[{"sha":"f9b8878a4fd814332650e3e2db8723cd3a58e2c8","author":{"email":"75d0c4ad517d85c3745aa112e45f2e479cb18cc2@users.noreply.github.com","name":"regro-cf-autotick-bot"},"message":"Rebuild for numpy 2.0

TL;DR: The way we build against numpy has changed as of numpy 2.0. This bot
PR has updated the recipe to account for the changes (see below for details).

The biggest change is that we no longer need to use the oldest available numpy
version at build time in order to support old numpy version at runtime - numpy
will by default use a compatible ABI for the oldest still-supported numpy versions.

Additionally, we no longer need to use `{{ pin_compatible("numpy") }}` as a
run requirement - this has been handled for more than two years now by a
run-export on the numpy package itself. The migrator will therefore remove
any occurrences of this.

However, you will still need to add the lower bound for the numpy version,
in line with what the upstream package requires. The default lower bound from
the run-export is `>=1.19`; if your package needs a newer version than that,
please add `numpy >=x.y` under `run:`.

Finally, by default, building against numpy 2.0 will assume that the package
is compatible with numpy 2.0, which is not necessarily the case. You should
check that the upstream package explicitly supports numpy 2.0, otherwise you
need to add a `- numpy <2.0dev0` run requirement until that happens (check numpy
issue 26191 for an overview of the most important packages).

### To-Dos:
  * [ ] Match run-requirements for numpy (i.e. check upstream `pyproject.toml` or however the project specifies numpy compatibility)
    * If upstream is not yet compatible with numpy 2.0, add `numpy <2.0dev0` upper bound under `run:`.
    * If upstream is already compatible with numpy 2.0, double-check their supported numpy versions.
    * If upstream requires a minimum numpy version newer than 1.19, you need to add `numpy >=x.y` under `run:`.
  * [ ] Remove any remaining occurrences of `{{ pin_compatible("numpy") }}` that the bot may have missed.

PS. If the build does not compile anymore, this is almost certainly a sign that
the upstream project is not yet ready for numpy 2.0; do not close this PR until
a version compatible with numpy 2.0 has been released upstream and on this
feedstock (in the meantime, you can keep the bot from reopening this PR in
case of git conflicts by marking it as a draft).","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/conda-forge/aligator-feedstock/commits/f9b8878a4fd814332650e3e2db8723cd3a58e2c8"},{"sha":"1892429de6905f0b6b4a996fab5ea17df8180657","author":{"email":"75d0c4ad517d85c3745aa112e45f2e479cb18cc2@users.noreply.github.com","name":"regro-cf-autotick-bot"},"message":"MNT: Re-rendered with conda-build 24.9.0, conda-smithy 3.43.0, and conda-forge-pinning 2024.10.25.13.38.33","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/conda-forge/aligator-feedstock/commits/1892429de6905f0b6b4a996fab5ea17df8180657"},{"sha":"6a0d6963a79c08e38bb7cb61b7ceeb2a13104cba","author":{"email":"d40d6df22368c8380fb0038562cab74a4e7e3bc8@inria.fr","name":"Joris Vaillant"},"message":"Update Numpy path","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/conda-forge/aligator-feedstock/commits/6a0d6963a79c08e38bb7cb61b7ceeb2a13104cba"},{"sha":"33e3143157e2f53b100eab9d52fe15d60aa51280","author":{"email":"d40d6df22368c8380fb0038562cab74a4e7e3bc8@inria.fr","name":"Joris Vaillant"},"message":"Avoid linking issue with last eigenpy","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/conda-forge/aligator-feedstock/commits/33e3143157e2f53b100eab9d52fe15d60aa51280"},{"sha":"f7ec66aff329f8b2a43db476dbcf6028000a78a9","author":{"email":"d40d6df22368c8380fb0038562cab74a4e7e3bc8@inria.fr","name":"Joris Vaillant"},"message":"Merge pull request #31 from regro-cf-autotick-bot/rebuild-numpy2-0-1_h5a5f86

PR switch to the new recipe format and use `rattler-build` as build tool. See: https://prefix.dev/blog/rattler_build_on_conda_forge This enables a faster build, more verbose and clearer package build output, and provides new features for better multi-output recipes (even if not used here, will be relevant for other packages that we wight migrate too). Main differences from initial conda recipe format are documented here: https://prefix-dev.github.io/rattler-build/latest/converting_from_conda_build/#converting-a-recipe-from-conda-build Main changes in the new recipe format in our concern : - Selectors are not anymore in comment form (e.g. `# [unix]`) but YAML form, using some `if / then / else` keywords - Python selectors have to be replaced with `match` function ( https://prefix-dev.github.io/rattler-build/latest/selectors/#the-match-function ) - Jinja expressions have to be prefixed with `$` (e.g. `${{ version }}`) - Tests section structuration - Some keywords changes in the `about` section - Default build scripts used now have a unified name : `build.sh` for Unix and `build.bat` for Windows, instead of `build.sh` and `bld.bat` respectively. - `min_pin` and `max_pin` in pinning expression are replaced by `lower_bound` and `upper_bound` respectively - `run_exports` have been moved in the lower part of `requirements` section for consistency with common practices (see examples of rattler-build https://github.com/prefix-dev/rattler-build/tree/main/examples ) Checklist * [x] Used a [personal fork of the feedstock to propose changes](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/updating_pkgs.html#forking-and-pull-requests) * [x] Bumped the build number * [x] [Re-rendered]( https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/updating_pkgs.html#rerendering-feedstocks ) with the latest `conda-smithy` (Use the phrase @conda-forge-admin, please rerender in a comment in this PR for automated rerendering) * [x] Ensured the license file is being packaged. PR switch to the new recipe format and use `rattler-build` as build tool. See: https://prefix.dev/blog/rattler_build_on_conda_forge This enables a faster build, more verbose and clearer package build output, and provides new features for better multi-output recipes (even if not used here, will be relevant for other packages that we wight migrate too). Main differences from initial conda recipe format are documented here: https://prefix-dev.github.io/rattler-build/latest/converting_from_conda_build/#converting-a-recipe-from-conda-build Main changes in the new recipe format in our concern : - Selectors are not anymore in comment form (e.g. `# [unix]`) but YAML form, using some `if / then / else` keywords - Python selectors have to be replaced with `match` function ( https://prefix-dev.github.io/rattler-build/latest/selectors/#the-match-function ) - Jinja expressions have to be prefixed with `$` (e.g. `${{ version }}`) - Tests section structuration - Some keywords changes in the `about` section - Default build scripts used now have a unified name : `build.sh` for Unix and `build.bat` for Windows, instead of `build.sh` and `bld.bat` respectively. - `min_pin` and `max_pin` in pinning expression are replaced by `lower_bound` and `upper_bound` respectively - `run_exports` have been moved in the lower part of `requirements` section for consistency with common practices (see examples of rattler-build https://github.com/prefix-dev/rattler-build/tree/main/examples ) Checklist * [x] Used a [personal fork of the feedstock to propose changes](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/updating_pkgs.html#forking-and-pull-requests) * [x] Bumped the build number * [x] [Re-rendered]( https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/updating_pkgs.html#rerendering-feedstocks ) with the latest `conda-smithy` (Use the phrase @conda-forge-admin, please rerender in a comment in this PR for automated rerendering) * [x] Ensured the license file is being packaged. 62","number":162,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"Switch format","user":{"login":"olivier-roussel","id":2340432,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIzNDA0MzI=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2340432?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/olivier-roussel","html_url":"https://github.com/olivier-roussel","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/olivier-roussel/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/olivier-roussel/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/olivier-roussel/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/olivier-roussel/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/olivier-roussel/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/olivier-roussel/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/olivier-roussel/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/olivier-roussel/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/olivier-roussel/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"This PR switch to the new recipe format and use `rattler-build` as build tool. See: https://prefix.dev/blog/rattler_build_on_conda_forge This enables a faster build, more verbose and clearer package build output, and provides new features for better multi-output recipes (even if not used here, will be relevant for other packages that we wight migrate too). Main differences from initial conda recipe format are documented here: https://prefix-dev.github.io/rattler-build/latest/converting_from_conda_build/#converting-a-recipe-from-conda-build Main changes in the new recipe format in our concern : - Selectors are not anymore in comment form (e.g. `# [unix]`) but YAML form, using some `if / then / else` keywords - Python selectors have to be replaced with `match` function ( https://prefix-dev.github.io/rattler-build/latest/selectors/#the-match-function ) - Jinja expressions have to be prefixed with `$` (e.g. `${{ version }}`) - Tests section structuration - Some keywords changes in the `about` section - Default build scripts used now have a unified name : `build.sh` for Unix and `build.bat` for Windows, instead of `build.sh` and `bld.bat` respectively. - `min_pin` and `max_pin` in pinning expression are replaced by `lower_bound` and `upper_bound` respectively - `run_exports` have been moved in the lower part of `requirements` section for consistency with common practices (see examples of rattler-build https://github.com/prefix-dev/rattler-build/tree/main/examples ) Checklist * [x] Used a [personal fork of the feedstock to propose changes](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/updating_pkgs.html#forking-and-pull-requests) * [x] Bumped the build number * [x] [Re-rendered]( https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/updating_pkgs.html#rerendering-feedstocks ) with the latest `conda-smithy` (Use the phrase @conda-forge-admin, please rerender in a comment in this PR for automated rerendering) * [x] Ensured the license file is being packaged. PR switch to the new recipe format and use `rattler-build` as build tool. See: https://prefix.dev/blog/rattler_build_on_conda_forge This enables a faster build, more verbose and clearer package build output, and provides new features for better multi-output recipes (even if not used here, will be relevant for other packages that we wight migrate too). Main differences from initial conda recipe format are documented here: https://prefix-dev.github.io/rattler-build/latest/converting_from_conda_build/#converting-a-recipe-from-conda-build Main changes in the new recipe format in our concern : - Selectors are not anymore in comment form (e.g. `# [unix]`) but YAML form, using some `if / then / else` keywords - Python selectors have to be replaced with `match` function ( https://prefix-dev.github.io/rattler-build/latest/selectors/#the-match-function ) - Jinja expressions have to be prefixed with `$` (e.g. `${{ version }}`) - Tests section structuration - Some keywords changes in the `about` section - Default build scripts used now have a unified name : `build.sh` for Unix and `build.bat` for Windows, instead of `build.sh` and `bld.bat` respectively. - `min_pin` and `max_pin` in pinning expression are replaced by `lower_bound` and `upper_bound` respectively - `run_exports` have been moved in the lower part of `requirements` section for consistency with common practices (see examples of rattler-build https://github.com/prefix-dev/rattler-build/tree/main/examples ) Checklist * [x] Used a [personal fork of the feedstock to propose changes](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/updating_pkgs.html#forking-and-pull-requests) * [x] Bumped the build number * [x] [Re-rendered]( https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/updating_pkgs.html#rerendering-feedstocks ) with the latest `conda-smithy` (Use the phrase @conda-forge-admin, please rerender in a comment in this PR for automated rerendering) * [x] Ensured the license file is being packaged. Rattler-build supports nested `if / then / else`  YAML conditionnal selectors, but conda smithy linter does not. 
The question of official support in new recipe format standard is still open (see https://github.com/conda-forge/conda-smithy/issues/2109 ). 
For now, we fix the linter by switching to one-lined selectors.

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| Rust            | `@conda-forge/help-rust`      |
| Go              | `@conda-forge/help-go`        |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io

All apologies in advance if your recipe PR does not receive prompt attention.
This is a high volume repository and the reviewers are volunteers. Review times
vary depending on the number of reviewers on a given language team and may be days
or weeks. We are always looking for more staged-recipe reviewers. If you are
interested in volunteering, please contact a member of @conda-forge/core.
We'd love to have the help!
-->

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [x] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [x] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).
- [x] GitHub users listed in the maintainer section have posted a comment confirming they are willing to be listed there.
- [x] When in trouble, please check our [knowledge base documentation](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html) before pinging a team.

Hi! This is the friendly automated conda-forge-webservice.

I've added user @ManifoldFR as instructed in #51.

Merge this PR to add the user. Please do not rerender this PR or change it in any way. It has `[ci skip]` in the commit message to avoid pushing a new build and so the build configuration in the feedstock should not be changed.

Please contact [conda-forge/core](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/maintainer_faq.html#mfaq-contact-core) to have this PR merged, if the maintainer is unresponsive.
